introSection: Wilde Maus XXL is a travelling steel wild mouse roller coaster built by German manufacturer Mack Rides currently owned by German showmen Max Eberhard & Sohn .

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Wilde Maus premiered in 1995. The roller coaster has operated on its own and with a mirror-image unit while travelling.

The roller coaster became the Wilde Maus XXL when the track was extended. It reopened in July 2012. In 2013, the supports were painted green and additional scenery was added.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Prior to its transformation, this was a Mack Rides Wilde Maus Compact Mobile roller coaster with the lift hill on the left hand side.

sections.title: Images
sections.content: The roller coaster at Walibi Lorraine.

fullText:
Wilde Maus XXL is a travelling steel wild mouse roller coaster built by German manufacturer Mack Rides currently owned by German showmen Max Eberhard & Sohn .

Wilde Maus premiered in 1995. The roller coaster has operated on its own and with a mirror-image unit while travelling.

The roller coaster became the Wilde Maus XXL when the track was extended. It reopened in July 2012. In 2013, the supports were painted green and additional scenery was added.

Prior to its transformation, this was a Mack Rides Wilde Maus Compact Mobile roller coaster with the lift hill on the left hand side.



The roller coaster at Walibi Lorraine.
